backlog.title: Lazy-Load Feature Delta — models load on first arm, unload after idle, teardown is bounded
backlog.description: Delta 003 against the already-implemented voice-typing project. Three cohesive changes with an explicit prerequisite chain: (1) bounded recorder teardown — fix the ~90s shutdown hang so idle-unload won't wedge every 30 min (PREREQUISITE); (2) lazy-load lifecycle — defer recorder construction to first arm, surface the lifecycle state machine in status/feedback; (3) idle-unload watchdog — reclaim VRAM after 30 min of disarmed idle. The system already boots eagerly building the recorder (~1.5-3 GB VRAM on every login); this delta makes it lazy so a boot where voice typing is never used consumes ~0 VRAM, while re-arms remain instant (models resident) until 30 min of disarmed idle unloads them. Research findings: plan/003_27d1f88f5a9f/architecture/.

milestones.title: Bounded Teardown (PREREQUISITE — unblocks M3 idle-unload; also fixes the existing quit hang)
milestones.description: The recorder.shutdown() call must complete in seconds, not ~90 s. Root cause (verified from RealtimeSTT v1.0.2 source): shutdown_recorder() in core/shutdown.py calls .join() WITHOUT timeouts on recording_thread and realtime_thread (daemon threads). If either thread is blocked in a non-cancellable operation (queue.get, GPU inference, C extension), the join hangs indefinitely. The two mp.Process spawns (transcript_process, reader_process) DO have 10s timeouts + terminate. Fix: wrap recorder.shutdown() in a hard timeout thread + force-clean the spawn processes to release VRAM. Also add TimeoutStopSec=15 to the systemd unit. This milestone MUST complete before M3 (idle-unload would trigger the 90s hang every 30 min otherwise).

tasks.title: Make VoiceTypingDaemon.shutdown() bounded — hard timeout + force-cleanup
tasks.description: Replace the current unbounded self._recorder.shutdown() call in VoiceTypingDaemon.shutdown() (daemon.py:817-840) with a bounded teardown: run shutdown in a background thread with a hard timeout (~10s); if it exceeds the budget, force-terminate transcript_process and reader_process (the mp.Process spawns that hold CUDA contexts / VRAM) so VRAM is released and the call returns. Keep idempotent + best-effort (don't re-raise). Also guard shutdown() for self._recorder is None (lazy-load readiness, M2).

subtasks.title: Root-cause the ~90s wedge and document findings
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The root cause is already documented in plan/003_27d1f88f5a9f/architecture/realtimestt_shutdown_analysis.md. RealtimeSTT v1.0.2's shutdown_recorder() (core/shutdown.py) calls .join() WITHOUT timeouts on recording_thread and realtime_thread. These are daemon threads, but .join() blocks regardless. The two mp.Process spawns (transcript_process, reader_process) have join(timeout=10) + terminate() — bounded. The 90s matches systemd's default TimeoutStopSec.
2. INPUT: RealtimeSTT source at .venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/RealtimeSTT/core/shutdown.py (already analyzed). The existing VoiceTypingDaemon.shutdown() method at daemon.py:817-840 which calls self._recorder.shutdown() with NO timeout.
3. LOGIC: Verify the analysis by reading the installed shutdown.py + initialization.py. Confirm: (a) recording_thread.join() has no timeout; (b) realtime_thread.join() has no timeout; (c) the two processes DO have timeouts. Record which thread/process blocks in practice by adding a temporary timed-log around each shutdown step and running `voicectl quit` on a live daemon (or skip the live test if the analysis is conclusive — the source code IS the proof). Write findings to plan/003_27d1f88f5a9f/architecture/ as confirmation.
4. OUTPUT: A confirmed root-cause document. No code changes in this subtask.
5. DOCS: none — research-only subtask, no user-facing/config/API surface change.

subtasks.title: Implement bounded teardown: hard timeout + force-cleanup of worker processes
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_27d1f88f5a9f/architecture/realtimestt_shutdown_analysis.md §Mitigation. The recorder's spawn-started mp.Process objects (transcript_process, reader_process) hold the CUDA contexts / VRAM. Terminating them releases VRAM immediately. The daemon threads (recording_thread, realtime_thread) cannot be killed but are daemon=True so they die with the process.
2. INPUT: The existing VoiceTypingDaemon.shutdown() method at daemon.py:817-840. It is guarded by a _shutdown_done getattr-flag + self._lock, calls self._recorder.shutdown(), logs success/failure, is defensive (swallows exceptions).
3. LOGIC: Implement a _bounded_shutdown(self, timeout=10.0) method that: (a) launches self._recorder.shutdown() in a daemon thread; (b) waits up to `timeout` seconds via threading.Event; (c) if it completes — great; (d) if it times out — force-terminate transcript_process and reader_process (check .is_alive() then .terminate()), set self._recorder.is_shut_down=True (idempotency), delete model references (realtime_transcription_model=None), and log a WARNING. Replace shutdown()'s current self._recorder.shutdown() call with self._bounded_shutdown(). Also add `if self._recorder is not None:` guard (preparation for M2 lazy load where recorder may never have been built). Keep idempotent via _shutdown_done flag. Keep defensive (best-effort, never re-raise). RealtimeSTT attributes to access: self._recorder.transcript_process, self._recorder.reader_process, self._recorder.is_shut_down, self._recorder.realtime_transcription_model. The existing _StubRecorder test fake (test_daemon.py) has .shutdowns counter — the bounded version should still increment it on the normal (fast) path.
4. OUTPUT: Modified shutdown() that completes in ≤timeout seconds even if RealtimeSTT hangs. The test suite's _StubRecorder/_RaisingRecorder fakes should still pass (they complete instantly). Add a test with a FakeSlowRecorder whose shutdown() blocks forever, asserting _bounded_shutdown force-cleans and returns within the timeout.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the shutdown() docstring (daemon.py:818-830) to describe the bounded behavior + force-cleanup. Update the module-top comment at daemon.py:11 and daemon.py:409 that currently tags shutdown as 'P1.M4.T2.S2'/'LATER' — bounded teardown is now implemented, not deferred.

tasks.title: Bound systemd stop — add TimeoutStopSec + verify bounded quit
tasks.description: Add TimeoutStopSec=15 to systemd/voice-typing.service so systemd never waits the full default 90s. The daemon's own bounded teardown (M1.T1) makes this safe. Currently the unit has neither TimeoutStopSec nor ExecStop.

subtasks.title: Add TimeoutStopSec=15 to systemd unit and verify no 90s SIGKILL
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: systemd/voice-typing.service currently has NO TimeoutStopSec → default 90s applies → the exact hang the PRD calls out (journal: 'Failed with result timeout'). The existing test_systemd_unit.py already greps the unit file for ExecStart/Restart — add TimeoutStopSec assertions there.
2. INPUT: systemd/voice-typing.service (the [Service] section has ExecStartPre, ExecStart, Restart=on-failure, RestartSec=2). tests/test_systemd_unit.py (existing tests that grep the unit file).
3. LOGIC: Add `TimeoutStopSec=15` to the [Service] section of systemd/voice-typing.service (after RestartSec=2). Add a comment explaining the daemon's own _bounded_shutdown makes this safe. Update test_systemd_unit.py to assert the unit contains TimeoutStopSec=15. The 15s budget gives the daemon's 10s _bounded_shutdown + 5s grace.
4. OUTPUT: Modified systemd unit + updated test. The install.sh script does not need changes — it already copies the unit file.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The TimeoutStopSec comment in the .service file IS the documentation. No README change here (the README teardown note is in M3.T3 — Mode B changeset sweep).

milestones.title: Lazy-Load Lifecycle (D1, D2, D3) — defer construction to first arm, surface lifecycle state
milestones.description: Remove the boot-time build_recorder() from VoiceTypingDaemon.__init__ (daemon.py:447); start with self._recorder = None. The start/toggle control-socket handlers acquire a single-flight load. The main loop idles while _recorder is None (matches PRD §4.2(1) pseudocode). Add the lifecycle state machine (unloaded/loading/loaded) and surface it in feedback (models_loaded) + status (phase/models_loaded). The arm command blocks during load and voicectl prints a 'loading models…' hint. Construction-failure CPU fallback migrates from main() into the lazy loader.

tasks.title: Defer recorder construction to first arm with single-flight load + CPU-fallback migration
tasks.description: Extract a _load_recorder() single-flight method (lock-guarded, no double-load, no half-built recorder on failure). Wire start/toggle to await it before arming. The main loop idles while _recorder is None. Migrate the construction-failure CPU fallback from main() into the lazy loader. Guard all 8 self._recorder.* call sites for None.

subtasks.title: Extract _load_recorder() single-flight method + guard all recorder call sites + migrate CPU fallback
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_27d1f88f5a9f/architecture/codebase_analysis.md §1.5 for the table of ALL 8 self._recorder.* call sites that need None guards: run():set_microphone(False), run():text(on_final), _arm():set_microphone(True), _disarm():set_microphone(False), _maybe_auto_stop():abort(), stop():abort(), toggle():abort(), request_shutdown():abort(), shutdown():shutdown(). The eager construction is at daemon.py:447-451: `self._recorder = recorder if recorder is not None else build_recorder(cfg, feedback, self._latency, on_speech=self._touch_speech)`. The construction-failure CPU fallback is in main() at daemon.py:~1301-1335.
2. INPUT: VoiceTypingConfig (cfg), Feedback (feedback), LatencyLog (self._latency), the build_recorder() factory (daemon.py:300-315), the _construct() testable seam (daemon.py:276-296), cuda_check.CPU_FALLBACK, _resolve_device_config().
3. LOGIC: (a) In __init__, replace the eager build with `self._recorder = recorder if recorder is not None else None` (None = lazy, not yet loaded). Add `self._models_loaded = False`, `self._loading = False`, `self._load_error: str | None = None` state attributes. (b) Add `_load_recorder() -> bool` method: acquires self._lock; if already loaded or loading → returns True/False without double-load; sets self._loading=True + feedback phase='loading'; releases lock during the heavy build_recorder() call (so concurrent status/toggle don't wedge); re-acquires lock to install self._recorder; on success sets self._models_loaded=True, feedback models_loaded=True, phase='idle'; on failure: retries with force_cpu=True (the existing CPU-fallback logic from main()), if both fail sets self._load_error, returns False, leaves no half-built recorder (self._recorder stays None). Returns True on success. (c) Wire _arm() to call `if not self._models_loaded: if not self._load_recorder(): return  # load failed, stay unarmed` before set_microphone(True). (d) Guard all 8 call sites: wrap recorder.set_microphone/abort/text/shutdown in `if self._recorder is not None:`. (e) In run(), skip the boot-time set_microphone(False) when _recorder is None; start the _idle_watchdog regardless (it no-ops when not listening). (f) Migrate the CPU-fallback from main() — remove the try/except around VoiceTypingDaemon(cfg, feedback, latency) construction and the build_recorder(force_cpu=True) retry block; _load_recorder() now owns this. main() just constructs the daemon (fast, no models) and calls run().
4. OUTPUT: A daemon that boots with self._recorder = None, ~0 VRAM, phase='unloaded'. The first voicectl start/toggle triggers _load_recorder() (~1-3s), then arms. The second arm is instant (recorder resident). A load failure returns False from _load_recorder(), the arm is suppressed, _load_error is set. The test fakes (_StubRecorder etc.) still work — tests that pre-inject a recorder via recorder= get a loaded daemon immediately (self._recorder != None, self._models_loaded = True when recorder is injected).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the module-top comment (daemon.py:11-52) and __init__ docstring (daemon.py:~411) that currently say 'build_recorder() once in __init__' / 'construct once' → 'built lazily on first arm (§4.2bis)'. Update the CONSTRUCT ONCE section in the module docstring.

subtasks.title: Thread the 'loading models…' hint through ctl.py + status during first arm
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The delta PRD §4.2bis says 'the arm command blocks during load and voicectl prints a loading models… hint.' Currently voicectl sends {"cmd":"start"} and reads one JSON line back — if _load_recorder() blocks for ~1-3s inside _arm()/start(), the voicectl client just waits. The status_snapshot() needs to report the loading phase so voicectl status (and the state.json) reflect it.
2. INPUT: ctl.py:format_result() (the status renderer at ctl.py:40-90), ControlServer._dispatch() (daemon.py:~1009-1039 which calls daemon.start()/toggle() then returns status_snapshot()).
3. LOGIC: (a) status_snapshot() (daemon.py:768) must include 'phase' and 'models_loaded' from the lifecycle state machine. When _recorder is None: phase='unloaded', models_loaded=False. During load: phase='loading', models_loaded=False. When loaded: phase='idle'/'listening'/'speaking', models_loaded=True. Also include 'load_error' (str|None) when a load failed. (b) In ctl.py format_result() for cmd='status', add a line: if models_loaded=False → print 'models: not loaded' or 'models: loading…' or 'models: load failed (error)' depending on phase; if models_loaded=True → keep the existing 'models: final_model + realtime_model' line. (c) For cmd='start'/'toggle' responses (which include status_snapshot), format_result already returns 'listening: on/off' — if phase='loading', augment to 'listening: on (loading models…)' so the user sees feedback. (d) The ControlServer._dispatch for 'start'/'toggle' returns {"ok":True, **status_snapshot()} AFTER _arm() completes — so if _load_recorder() succeeded, the response carries models_loaded=True. If load failed, _arm() is suppressed and the response carries ok:True but listening:off + load_error. (Actually _dispatch returns ok:True + status — consider returning ok:False when load fails so format_result shows the error. OR keep ok:True + a 'load_error' key and let format_result display it.)
4. OUTPUT: voicectl start prints 'listening: on' with models info if loaded, or an error hint if load failed. voicectl status shows the live phase (unloaded/loading/idle/listening/speaking) and models_loaded.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The ctl.py format_result docstring should document the new 'loading models…' / 'not loaded' / 'load failed' status lines.

tasks.title: Lifecycle state machine + feedback wiring — phase/models_loaded in state.json and status
tasks.description: Add models_loaded:bool and unloaded/loading phases to the Feedback state (feedback.py:85-88). Update the boot phase to 'unloaded' (not 'idle'). Wire daemon lifecycle transitions to feedback state writes. Surface phase + models_loaded in status_snapshot() and ctl.py status rendering.

subtasks.title: Expose phase + models_loaded from feedback, status_snapshot(), and ctl status rendering
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: feedback.py:85-88 _state dict currently has keys: listening, phase, partial, last_final, ts. Phase cycles idle/listening/speaking. The boot phase is 'idle' (feedback.py:86). status_snapshot() (daemon.py:768) returns listening/partial/last_final/uptime_s/device/compute_type/final_model/realtime_model/mic_ok/mic_error — NO phase, NO models_loaded. ctl.py format_result() renders these fields.
2. INPUT: Feedback.__init__ _state (feedback.py:76-82), Feedback.set_phase() (feedback.py:~108), Feedback.set_listening() (feedback.py:~131), Feedback.snapshot() (feedback.py:~155), VoiceTypingDaemon.status_snapshot() (daemon.py:768), ctl.py format_result() (ctl.py:40-90).
3. LOGIC: (a) In feedback.py: add 'models_loaded': False to _state. Change the boot 'phase' from 'idle' to 'unloaded'. Add a set_models_loaded(bool) method that updates _state['models_loaded'] and writes (always write — lifecycle transitions are infrequent). Add 'loading' to the valid phase values (just a string, no validation needed — set_phase accepts any string). The existing set_phase/set_listening/update_partial/record_final methods stay unchanged. (b) In daemon.py: call self._feedback.set_phase('unloaded') at construction (not just in run()). When _load_recorder() starts, call set_phase('loading') + set_models_loaded(False). On success: set_phase('idle') + set_models_loaded(True). On failure: set_phase('unloaded') + set_models_loaded(False). (c) In status_snapshot(): add 'phase' and 'models_loaded' and 'load_error' to the returned dict. Read phase/models_loaded from self._feedback.snapshot() (they're in _state now). Read load_error from self._load_error. (d) In ctl.py format_result() for status: add the models_loaded/phase rendering (augment or replace the existing 'models:' line from S2). (e) Update the test test_status_snapshot_keys_and_cuda_values (test_daemon.py:~590) to assert the new keys.
4. OUTPUT: state.json now includes models_loaded and uses unloaded/loading/idle/listening/speaking phases. voicectl status shows the live lifecycle. The tmux status.sh helper reads phase/models_loaded from state.json (no change needed to status.sh itself — it already reads the JSON).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update feedback.py:10 module-top state-shape comment to include models_loaded and the new phase values. Update the _state dict docstring to document the boot state unloaded/models_loaded:false.

milestones.title: Idle Unload + Config + T6 Test + Docs (D4, D7) — VRAM reclamation after disarmed idle
milestones.description: Depends on M1 (bounded teardown) and M2 (lifecycle exists to tear down). Add the auto_unload_idle_seconds config knob (default 1800s = 30 min). Add an _idle_unload_watchdog thread modeled on the existing _idle_watchdog. When the recorder has sat loaded/not-listening for the threshold, tear it down via the M1 bounded teardown → unloaded state. Rewrite T6 as the 4-part lazy-load lifecycle test. Sync README for the new knob + lazy-load behavior.

tasks.title: Idle-unload watchdog + auto_unload_idle_seconds config knob
tasks.description: Add the config knob + a watchdog thread that tears down the recorder after auto_unload_idle_seconds of disarmed idle. The clock starts when the mic disarms, resets on any arm. Teardown uses the M1 bounded shutdown under the same single-flight lock as load.

subtasks.title: Add config knob (config.py + config.toml) + idle-unload watchdog thread + composition with auto-stop
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The existing _idle_watchdog (daemon.py:650-659) is the TEMPLATE: it ticks via self._shutdown.wait(1.0), calls _maybe_auto_stop() which re-checks the deadline under self._lock. The existing _maybe_auto_stop() (daemon.py:581-649) is the template for 'check a deadline under the lock, perform a guarded transition.' The idle auto-stop (auto_stop_idle_seconds, default 30s) disarms the mic but does NOT unload — it should START the idle-unload clock (or let the idle-unload watchdog independently track when the mic was last disarmed). The config.py AsrConfig (config.py:46-55) has auto_stop_idle_seconds but NOT auto_unload_idle_seconds.
2. INPUT: config.py AsrConfig dataclass (add field), config.toml [asr] section (add line with comment), daemon.py _idle_watchdog pattern, daemon.py _maybe_auto_stop pattern, VoiceTypingDaemon._bounded_shutdown() from P1.M1.T1.S2, VoiceTypingDaemon._load_recorder() from P1.M2.T1.S1, self._lock, self._shutdown, self._models_loaded.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add `auto_unload_idle_seconds: float = 1800.0` to AsrConfig (config.py). (b) Add a commented line to config.toml [asr] section after auto_stop_idle_seconds: `auto_unload_idle_seconds = 1800.0  # after this many seconds disarmed (loaded, not listening), tear down models to free VRAM; 0 disables (§4.2bis Idle unload)`. (c) Add `_idle_unload_watchdog()` method to VoiceTypingDaemon: ticks via self._shutdown.wait(1.0) (same as _idle_watchdog); tracks when the mic was last disarmed (a new `self._disarmed_monotonic: float | None` — set when _disarm() is called, cleared when _arm() is called); when self._models_loaded AND not self._listening.is_set() AND now - self._disarmed_monotonic >= cfg.asr.auto_unload_idle_seconds → call self._unload_recorder(). threshold<=0 disables. (d) Add `_unload_recorder()` method: acquires self._lock (same lock as _load_recorder — single-flight so a racing arm waits); if not self._models_loaded → return; call self._bounded_shutdown() (from M1); set self._recorder = None; self._models_loaded = False; feedback.set_phase('unloaded') + set_models_loaded(False); log 'voice-typing idle-unload: 1800.0s disarmed; unloading models'. (e) Start the watchdog thread from run() alongside _idle_watchdog. (f) Ensure composition with auto-stop: auto-stop disarms at 30s → sets _disarmed_monotonic → idle-unload watchdog counts from there → unloads at 30s+1800s = ~30.5 min. (g) Guard: an arm arriving during _unload_recorder() waits on _lock (the unload holds it), then _load_recorder() runs fresh — tested in S2.
4. OUTPUT: After 30 min of disarmed idle, nvidia-smi shows the daemon PID gone (~0 VRAM). A subsequent voicectl start reloads (~1-3s). Config auto_unload_idle_seconds=0 disables.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The auto_unload_idle_seconds comment in config.toml IS the documentation. Update config.py AsrConfig docstring to mention the new field.

subtasks.title: Verify teardown-vs-load race safety — arm racing idle-unload teardown waits then loads fresh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The delta PRD §4.2bis says 'Teardown (idle-unload and quit) runs under the SAME single-flight lock as load, so an arm that races an in-flight teardown waits for it, then loads fresh — an arm can never see a half-torn-down recorder.' Both _load_recorder() and _unload_recorder() acquire self._lock.
2. INPUT: _load_recorder() (P1.M2.T1.S1), _unload_recorder() (P1.M3.T1.S1), self._lock.
3. LOGIC: Verify (via unit test in test_daemon.py using the _StubRecorder fake): (a) simulate idle-unload in progress (hold self._lock from a test thread, call _unload_recorder from another, then call start from a third) → the start blocks until _unload_recorder releases _lock, then _load_recorder() runs. (b) After the race resolves: self._recorder is a fresh instance, self._models_loaded is True, listening is on. (c) No half-torn-down recorder is ever visible (self._recorder is either None or a fully-built instance, never partially constructed). (d) The _bounded_shutdown during unload does not block the arm excessively (the arm waits for it but the timeout is bounded). These are unit tests using fakes — no real CUDA.
4. OUTPUT: A test proving the race is safe. No additional code changes beyond what S1 provides — this subtask VERIFIES the design.
5. DOCS: none — test-only subtask, no user-facing/config/API surface change.

tasks.title: Rewrite T6 as GPU-lifecycle (lazy load) + fast pytest regression
tasks.description: Convert tests/test_idle_and_gpu.sh T6 from residency assertion to the 4-part lifecycle assertion: (a) idle/never-armed → daemon PID absent from nvidia-smi; (b) armed → PID + ~1-5 GB; (c) disarmed → still resident; (d) disarmed + idle ≥ auto_unload_idle_seconds → PID gone, reload reappears. Add fast pytest unit tests for the lifecycle state machine + single-flight.

subtasks.title: Fast pytest: lifecycle transitions, single-flight load, load-failure, idle-unload fire/reset/disable
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The existing test_daemon.py uses a _StubRecorder fake (test_daemon.py:~360) and a _make_daemon() helper (test_daemon.py:~440) that injects fakes. The _StubRecorder has text(), set_microphone(), abort(), shutdown() methods. Tests pre-inject the recorder via the recorder= kwarg. For lazy-load tests, tests need a daemon WITHOUT a pre-injected recorder (recorder=None) so _load_recorder() can be exercised. The existing _idle_watchdog tests (test_daemon.py:~500-560) are the pattern for watchdog lifecycle tests.
2. INPUT: tests/test_daemon.py (existing test suite with _StubRecorder/_DaemonFakeFeedback/_FakeBackend/_ok_probe/_make_daemon patterns), VoiceTypingDaemon with lazy-load (_recorder=None boot, _load_recorder(), _unload_recorder(), _models_loaded state).
3. LOGIC: Add tests to test_daemon.py (ADDITIVE — do not change existing tests). Tests needed: (a) boot: _make_daemon() WITHOUT recorder= injection → daemon._recorder is None, _models_loaded is False, phase='unloaded'. (b) start triggers _load_recorder: inject a FAKE recorder builder (monkeypatch daemon.build_recorder to return a _StubRecorder) → call daemon.start() → _recorder is not None, _models_loaded is True, is_listening() is True. (c) single-flight: two concurrent start() calls → build_recorder called exactly ONCE. (d) load failure → unloaded: monkeypatch build_recorder to raise → start() does not arm, _recorder stays None, _load_error is set, status reports ok:false or load_error. (e) idle-unload fires: set _disarmed_monotonic to now - 1801s, call _maybe_idle_unload() → _recorder is None, _models_loaded is False. (f) idle-unload disabled when threshold=0: set auto_unload_idle_seconds=0 → _maybe_idle_unload() is a no-op. (g) arm resets the idle-unload clock. (h) existing tests that inject recorder= still work (they skip lazy load). IMPORTANT: the existing _make_daemon() passes recorder=_StubRecorder() — these tests should continue to work unchanged (the recorder= injection means _models_loaded should be set to True in __init__ when recorder is provided).
4. OUTPUT: A green fast pytest suite with new lifecycle + single-flight + idle-unload tests. Run: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_daemon.py -v.
5. DOCS: none — test-only subtask.

subtasks.title: Rewrite tests/test_idle_and_gpu.sh T6 section to 4-part lazy-load lifecycle assertions + confirm T4 still holds
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The current tests/test_idle_and_gpu.sh (PRD §6 T4+T6) asserts GPU RESIDENCY: after daemon boot + voicectl start, nvidia-smi shows the daemon PID with 1-5 GB VRAM, and it stays resident after stop. The existing test uses nvidia-smi --query-compute-apps=pid,used_memory, matches the daemon's process tree, and asserts total in [1024, 5120] MiB. With lazy-load, the assertions MUST change: (a) after boot with NO arm → PID ABSENT from nvidia-smi; (b) after voicectl start → PID present with VRAM; (c) after voicectl stop → still present; (d) after auto_unload_idle_seconds disarmed → PID gone. The test uses a config override via XDG_CONFIG_HOME pointing at a temp config.toml.
2. INPUT: tests/test_idle_and_gpu.sh (the existing T6 section that does nvidia-smi GPU residency checks + the T4 idle window + the voicectl exercise). The daemon_tree_pids() helper, the nvidia-smi query helper, the cleanup trap.
3. LOGIC: Modify the T6 section of test_idle_and_gpu.sh: (a) AFTER daemon boot + BEFORE voicectl start: assert daemon PID is ABSENT from nvidia-smi compute-apps (the lazy-load guarantee — ~0 VRAM at boot). (b) AFTER voicectl start: assert daemon PID is PRESENT with total VRAM in [1024, 5120] MiB (same as current). (c) AFTER voicectl stop: assert PID STILL PRESENT (models stay resident — instant re-arm). (d) For the idle-unload sub-test: set auto_unload_idle_seconds to a SHORT value (e.g. 5.0s) in the temp config.toml override → start → stop → wait 7s → assert PID GONE from nvidia-smi → start again → assert PID reappears (reload). IMPORTANT: the T4 idle-stability test (120s of silence) must still pass — its 120s window never triggers the 30-min idle-unload, so use the default 1800s for the T4 portion and override to a short value only for the T6(d) portion (or use a separate daemon run). The test structure: run T4 first (120s silence, default config), then T6 lifecycle assertions. For T6(d) specifically, the config override to auto_unload_idle_seconds=5 is needed — start a second daemon run or adjust the config dynamically. Consider running T6(d) as a separate daemon invocation with its own config override to avoid interfering with T4.
4. OUTPUT: A passing test_idle_and_gpu.sh that proves all 4 lazy-load lifecycle sub-assertions via real nvidia-smi output, plus T4 idle-stability still green. The evidence block includes nvidia-smi output for all 4 states.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the test_idle_and_gpu.sh header comment to document the new T6 lifecycle assertions. Update tests/ACCEPTANCE.md if it references the old T6 residency assertion.

subtasks.title: Apply README edits: config table, GPU lifecycle section, First-run loading note, bounded quit note, stale phrasing sweep
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: README.md (~13KB, ~280+ lines) currently describes boot-time GPU residency. The delta PRD §3 Mode B says: (a) add auto_unload_idle_seconds to the config-tuning table (README.md:~132 neighborhood, near auto_stop_idle_seconds); (b) rewrite the 'Confirm the models are resident' GPU section (README.md:~283) to the lazy-load lifecycle: ~0 VRAM at boot, ~1-3s on first arm, resident until quit or 30-min disarmed idle, reload on next arm; (c) add a 'First arm loads models (~1-3s, one-time per session)' note to the First-run section + the loading models… voicectl hint; (d) note the bounded quit teardown (no more ~90s systemd timeout). If install.sh prints a GPU-residency blurb, align it too.
2. INPUT: README.md (current), config.toml (the new auto_unload_idle_seconds line), install.sh (check for GPU-residency blurbs), the actual implemented behavior from M1+M2+M3.
3. LOGIC: (a) Config table: add a row for auto_unload_idle_seconds mirroring the auto_stop_idle_seconds row. (b) GPU section: rewrite from 'models load at boot, ~1.5-3 GB resident' to 'models load lazily on first voicectl start, ~0 VRAM at boot, ~1-3s load on first arm, resident until quit or 30-min idle, unload frees VRAM, next arm reloads.' (c) First-run: add 'Note: the first voicectl toggle/start each session takes ~1-3s to load models (voicectl shows loading models…); subsequent arms are instant.' (d) Troubleshooting: note that voicectl quit now completes in seconds (bounded teardown, no 90s hang). (e) Grep the repo for stale phrasing: 'resident', '2.8 GB', 'build recorder once', 'construct once', 'recorder resident at boot' — fix any remaining drift in README.md and voice_typing/*.py comments (but code-comment updates should have been done as Mode A in the implementing subtasks; this is a cross-check).
4. OUTPUT: A README.md that accurately describes the lazy-load lifecycle, the new config knob, the bounded quit, and the loading models… hint. No stale boot-time-residency claims remain.
5. DOCS: This IS the docs task (Mode B). All changeset-level documentation for this delta is done here.
